preamble: The shift from the back unrounded vowel [ʌ] to the front unrounded vowel [æ] (or its close relative [a]) is a stark example of vowel fronting. It often occurs as part of a chain shift where the front of the mouth 'empties out', pulling back vowels forward.

examples.note: Claimed as part of the California Vowel Shift, but published quantitative evidence for STRUT fronting specifically is limited; the CVS primarily documents low-back merger and back vowel fronting.
STRUT vowel is fronted and lowered in these dialects, often realized as [ɐ] in Australian English.
